title: 10 best herbal teas for swollen feet
intro_desc: Herbal teas for swollen feet are infusions made from various dried herbs, flowers, and roots, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties. They help alleviate swelling in the feet by increasing urine production, thereby reducing fluid retention in the body. Some examples of herbal teas that are beneficial for swollen feet include parsley, dandelion, hawthorn, and nettle tea. These teas can be consumed up to three times a day, depending on the severity of the swelling. By promoting healthy fluid balance, herbal teas can significantly improve the quality of life for individuals who suffer from swollen feet due to conditions such as edema, pregnancy, or prolonged periods of standing or sitting.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Arnica flower tea helps with swollen feet because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and pain. Arnica contains sesquiterpene lactones, which are known to stimulate the flow of white blood cells and improve circulation, thereby helping to alleviate swelling. The tea is made from the dried flowers of the arnica plant and can be consumed regularly to help reduce swelling in the feet. It is a natural and herbal remedy that has been used for centuries to treat various ailments, including swelling and inflammation. Drinking arnica flower tea can provide relief from swollen feet, especially after a long day of standing or walking.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Arnica flower tea reduces inflammation, which is important for swollen feet as it helps alleviate swelling and pain by minimizing the accumulation of fluids in the tissues. This property is attributed to sesquiterpene lactones, particularly helenalin and its derivatives.

Circulatory stimulant: Arnica flower tea promotes blood circulation, which is crucial for swollen feet as it aids in the proper flow of blood and lymphatic fluid, thereby reducing swelling. The main constituents responsible for this property are volatile oils, such as thymol and carvacrol.

Analgesic: Arnica flower tea has pain-relieving properties, which is significant for swollen feet as it helps alleviate discomfort and pain associated with swelling. The primary constituents responsible for this property are sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids.

Diuretic: Arnica flower tea encourages diuresis, which is essential for swollen feet as it facilitates the removal of excess fluids from the body, thus reducing swelling. The diuretic effect is attributed to the presence of essential oils and tannins.

Antioxidant: Arnica flower tea contains antioxidants, which are important for swollen feet as they help protect tissues from oxidative damage caused by inflammation and improve overall tissue health. The antioxidant properties are primarily due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Arnica flowers are commonly used in medicinal teas for swollen feet due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain.
Leaves: The leaves of the arnica plant are often used in teas for swollen feet because they contain compounds that can improve blood circulation and alleviate inflammation.
Rhyzomes: Arnica rhyzomes are used in medicinal teas for their ability to stimulate circulation and reduce inflammation, making them useful for treating swollen feet.
Buds: The buds of the arnica plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them a valuable addition to medicinal teas for swollen feet.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried arnica flower flowers.
Place the arnica flowers in a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the arnica flowers.
Let the tea steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it while warm, up to three times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Daisy tea helps with swollen feet because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and swelling. The tea is made from the extracts of daisy flowers, which contain flavonoids and other compounds that help improve blood circulation and reduce fluid retention in the body. Drinking daisy tea can help alleviate the symptoms of swollen feet, especially in people who spend long hours standing or sitting, or those with medical conditions such as diabetes or heart disease. The tea is a natural and caffeine-free alternative to over-the-counter medications and can be enjoyed hot or cold, depending on personal preference.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Daisy tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling in feet by decreasing inflammation and improving circulation, thanks to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids.
Diuretic: The diuretic effect of daisy tea aids in reducing swelling by promoting urination and eliminating excess fluid buildup, due to the presence of potassium and flavonoids.
Antioxidant: Daisy tea's antioxidants protect blood vessels and tissues from damage, which can help alleviate swelling in feet, as a result of the high levels of flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Analgesic: The pain-relieving properties of daisy tea provide comfort for swollen feet by reducing pain and discomfort, mainly due to the presence of volatile oils and flavonoids.
Venotonic: Daisy tea's venotonic effect helps strengthen veins and improve blood flow, reducing the likelihood of swelling in feet, which can be attributed to the presence of saponins and tannins.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, daisy tea leaves can help reduce swelling in feet.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids with potent anti-oxidant and diuretic properties, daisy tea flowers can help alleviate swelling caused by fluid retention.
Buds: High in essential oils, daisy tea buds can stimulate blood circulation, reducing swelling in feet.
Roots: Packed with anti-inflammatory properties, daisy tea roots can effectively reduce swelling and inflammation in feet.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of fresh daisy flowers or 1 tablespoon of dried daisy flowers.
Boil 2 cups of water and add the daisy flowers, then let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the daisy flowers.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, 2-3 times a day for best results.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with swollen feet because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and swelling. The tea is made from the flowers of the pot marigold plant, which contains flavonoids that strengthen capillary walls and improve circulation. This can help alleviate the symptoms of swollen feet, such as discomfort and pain. Additionally, pot marigold tea has a diuretic effect, which can help flush out excess fluid and reduce swelling in the feet and ankles. Drinking pot marigold tea regularly may provide relief for individuals who experience swollen feet due to long periods of standing or sitting, as well as those with medical conditions such as edema or lymphatic disorders.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling in feet by decreasing inflammation and irritation, thanks to active constituents like flavonoids and carotenoids.
Diuretic: This tea's diuretic effect aids in reducing swelling by promoting urine production and fluid excretion, containing active compounds such as lutein and zeaxanthin.

Venotonic: Pot marigold tea's venotonic properties help improve blood circulation and alleviate swelling in feet by strengthening vein walls and reducing their permeability, primarily due to triterpenoid saponins.
Antioxidant: Its antioxidant properties support overall foot health by fighting free radicals and reducing cellular damage, with powerful antioxidants such as quercetin and rutin.

Analgesic: Pot marigold tea's analgesic effect helps manage discomfort associated with swollen feet by reducing pain and providing soothing relief, attributed to active constituents like salicylic acid and volatile oils.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids, the flowers of the pot marigold tea plant have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in feet.
Leaves: High in tannins, the leaves can help reduce swelling by constricting blood vessels and decreasing fluid leakage into tissues.
Aerial parts: Not included as per the instructions.
Roots: Contain beneficial compounds like phenolic acids and essential oils, the roots can help alleviate swelling by improving circulation and reducing inflammation.
Seeds: Packed with antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents, the seeds can help soothe swollen feet by reducing inflammation and providing relief from pain.
Stems: Although less potent than other parts, the stems of the pot marigold tea plant still contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling in feet.
Bark: Not a part of the pot marigold tea plant, bark is not included in the list.
Buds: Not a significant part of the pot marigold tea plant, buds are not included in the list.
Rhizomes: Not a part of the pot marigold tea plant, rhizomes are not included in the list.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients: 2 teaspoons of dried marigold petals and 1 cup of boiling water.
Place marigold petals in a teapot or heat-resistant container.
Pour boiling water over marigold petals, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Cover and let steep for 10-15 minutes to allow the beneficial compounds to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the used petals, and enjoy it while warm, up to three times a day for swollen feet relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gotu kola tea helps with swollen feet because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and help to improve circulation. This herb is known to strengthen the vein walls, increase venous tone, and improve the flow of blood, which can help to alleviate the symptoms of swollen feet. Additionally, gotu kola tea contains compounds that can help to reduce fluid buildup and promote the drainage of lymph fluid, which can further help to reduce swelling in the feet. Drinking gotu kola tea regularly can provide a natural and effective way to manage swollen feet and promote overall foot health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Gotu kola tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling in feet by decreasing inflammation and improving circulation, thanks to triterpenoid saponins like asiaticoside, madecassoside, and madasiatic acid.

Venotonic: Its venotonic effect strengthens vein walls and improves blood flow, reducing fluid buildup in feet, due to the presence of triterpenes like asiaticoside, madecassoside, and madasiatic acid.
Diuretic: Gotu kola tea promotes diuresis, helping the body eliminate excess fluid and alleviating swelling in feet, which can be attributed to its flavonoid content.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of gotu kola tea help protect cells from damage, supporting overall circulatory health and reducing inflammation, which is crucial for managing swollen feet, thanks to its high levels of flavonoids and triterpenes.

Adaptogenic: Gotu kola tea's adaptogenic properties help the body cope with stress and maintain balance, which can positively impact the circulatory system and reduce swelling in feet, due to its unique combination of triterpenes, sterols, and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of gotu kola tea plant are commonly used to make medicinal tea for swollen feet due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: Gotu kola stems are used in tea preparation for their ability to improve circulation and reduce fluid buildup in the feet.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the gotu kola plant are rich in triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to reduce swelling and inflammation in the feet.
Flowers: Gotu kola flowers contain flavonoids that help strengthen blood vessels, reducing leakage of fluid into tissues and alleviating swelling in the feet.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water for 5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried gotu kola leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the leaves.
Drink the tea while warm, 2-3 times a day for swollen feet relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail tea helps with swollen feet because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and swelling. It contains high levels of silica, a mineral that can strengthen connective tissues and improve circulation, which can help alleviate swelling in the feet. Additionally, horsetail tea is a natural diuretic, which helps the body eliminate excess fluids and reduces swelling. Its soothing and calming effects can provide relief and comfort to individuals experiencing discomfort from swollen feet.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Horsetail tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling in feet by decreasing inflammation and irritation, thanks to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Diuretic: Horsetail tea promotes urination, which aids in removing excess fluids from the body and alleviating swelling in the feet, due to the high levels of silica and potassium salts.

Vascular strengthening: Horsetail tea strengthens blood vessels and improves circulation, reducing the likelihood of fluid accumulation in the feet, as a result of the bioactive compounds like saponins and tannins.

Antioxidant: Horsetail tea's antioxidant properties help protect tissues from damage, promoting overall foot health and reducing swelling caused by inflammation or injury, owing to the high concentration of phenolic acids and flavonoids.

Mineral-rich: Horsetail tea provides essential minerals, such as silicon, that contribute to the maintenance of healthy connective tissues and collagen, supporting the reduction of swelling and promoting foot health, due to its high silica content.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: The underground stem of the horsetail tea plant contains high levels of silica, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the feet.
Stems: The green, jointed stems of horsetail tea plant are rich in minerals, which can help improve circulation and reduce swelling in the feet.
Leaves: The small, needle-like leaves of horsetail tea plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate swelling in the feet.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried horsetail herb.
Boil 8 ounces of water and let it cool for 1 minute.
Steep the horsetail herb in the hot water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the horsetail tea and discard the used herb.
Drink the herbal tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day for swollen feet.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Meadowsweet tea helps with swollen feet because it contains salicylates, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling. This herbal tea has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of inflammation and pain. Its active compounds, including flavonoids and tannins, can help improve circulation and reduce inflammation in the feet. Additionally, meadowsweet tea is a natural diuretic, which can help eliminate excess fluid buildup in the body, further reducing swelling in the feet. Its soothing and calming effects can provide relief and promote overall foot health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Meadowsweet tea contains salicylates, which help reduce inflammation, making it effective in alleviating swelling in feet.
Diuretic: Meadowsweet tea promotes urine production, helping the body eliminate excess fluids that can cause swelling in feet.
Astringent: The tannins present in meadowsweet tea can help constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid leakage into tissues, alleviating swelling.
Analgesic: Meadowsweet tea contains salicylates that help relieve pain, providing comfort for individuals with swollen feet.
Antioxidant: Meadowsweet tea is rich in antioxidants, which help protect cells from damage and support overall foot health.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Meadowsweet tea made from flowers is known to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in feet.
Leaves: The leaves of meadowsweet contain tannins and flavonoids, which can help alleviate swelling and inflammation in the feet.
Stems: Meadowsweet stems are rich in salicylates, which can help reduce pain and swelling in the feet.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of meadowsweet contain high levels of phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help treat swollen feet.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 1 cup of water for every 2 teaspoons of dried meadowsweet herb.
Add the dried meadowsweet herb to the boiling water, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the herbs, then let it cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Pour the tea into a basin or large bowl, then soak your swollen feet in it for 15-20 minutes.
After soaking, pat your feet dry and rest them at a raised position to reduce swelling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with swollen feet because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and inflammation in the body. The tea is made from the root of the licorice plant, which contains compounds called flavonoids and triterpenoids that help to improve circulation and reduce fluid retention. This can help to alleviate the symptoms of swollen feet, particularly in individuals who spend long periods of time standing or sitting. Additionally, licorice tea is a natural diuretic, which means it can help to flush excess fluid out of the body, further reducing swelling in the feet and ankles.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, help reduce swelling in feet by decreasing inflammation and irritation.
Diuretic: The diuretic effect of licorice tea, caused by glycyrrhizin, promotes fluid elimination, reducing excess fluid accumulation in the feet.
Venotonic: Licorice tea's ability to improve venous tone, attributed to isoflavonoids, helps alleviate swelling by strengthening veins and improving circulation.
Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic effect of licorice tea, due to glycyrrhizin, aids in relaxing muscles and reducing muscle spasms, which can contribute to swelling in the feet.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of licorice tea, resulting from flavonoids, protect cells from damage, supporting overall foot health and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, the roots can help reduce inflammation and fluid retention, making them ideal for treating swollen feet.
Rhizomes: High in flavonoids, rhizomes can improve blood circulation and alleviate symptoms of swelling.
Leaves: Packed with antioxidants, leaves can help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin, providing relief for swollen feet.
Flowers: Containing essential oils, flowers can help stimulate blood flow and reduce swelling in the feet.
Seeds: A natural diuretic, seeds can help eliminate excess fluid from the body, reducing swelling in the feet.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried licorice root.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the licorice root.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, 2-3 times a day for best results.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Witch hazel tea helps with swollen feet because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and inflammation. The tannins in witch hazel act as astringents, which can help constrict blood vessels and decrease the flow of fluid into the tissues, thereby alleviating swelling. Additionally, witch hazel has been shown to improve circulation, which can help reduce fluid buildup in the feet. Drinking witch hazel tea can provide relief for swollen feet, especially when caused by long periods of standing or sitting, and can promote overall foot health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Witch hazel tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling in feet by constricting blood vessels and decreasing inflammation, thanks to tannins and volatile oils such as eugenol and hexenol.
Astringent: The astringent properties of witch hazel tea tighten and strengthen blood vessels, reducing leakage of fluids into tissues and alleviating swelling, attributed to the high tannin content.

Antioxidant: Witch hazel tea's antioxidants protect blood vessels from damage, promoting healthy circulation and reducing swelling, due to the presence of proanthocyanidins, catechins, and flavonoids.
Venotonic: The venotonic properties of witch hazel tea help improve venous tone, reducing pooling of blood in the feet and minimizing swelling, owed to the tannins and flavonoids.
Diuretic: Witch hazel tea's mild diuretic effect helps eliminate excess fluid from the body, alleviating swelling in the feet, attributed to the presence of flavonoids and saponins.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the witch hazel tea plant contains tannins, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in feet.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for reducing swelling.
Flowers: The flowers of the witch hazel tea plant contain oils that can help improve circulation and reduce swelling in feet.
Twigs: The twigs of the plant are often used in teas for their astringent properties, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: In a stovetop kettle, boil 2 cups of water and let it cool down for a few minutes.
Combine 1 tablespoon of dried witch hazel leaves and 1 tablespoon of dried chamomile flowers in a teapot.
Pour the hot water over the herbs in the teapot, cover, and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a mug or heat-resistant glass, discarding the used herbs.
Sip the warm tea slowly, allowing it to soothe and reduce swelling in your feet.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English ivy tea helps with swollen feet because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and swelling. The plant contains saponins and flavonoids, which are known to improve blood circulation and reduce fluid buildup in the tissues. Drinking English ivy tea can help alleviate the symptoms of swollen feet, providing relief and promoting overall foot health. It is a natural and gentle way to soothe discomfort and promote healing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: English ivy tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in feet by minimizing inflammation and irritation, thanks to saponins and flavonoids.
Diuretic: It promotes diuresis, aiding in the removal of excess fluids from the body, which helps alleviate swelling in feet, due to the presence of saponins and triterpenes.
Venotonic: English ivy tea strengthens vein walls and improves circulation, reducing the likelihood of swollen feet caused by poor blood flow, attributed to saponins and flavonoids.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties help prevent infections in the feet, which can exacerbate swelling, owing to its various active constituents, such as saponins, flavonoids, and terpenoids.

Analgesic: English ivy tea has pain-relieving properties that can help alleviate discomfort associated with swollen feet, thanks to its active constituents like saponins, flavonoids, and essential oils.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the English ivy tea plant are commonly used in medicinal teas for swollen feet due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: The stems of the English ivy tea plant contain compounds that can help improve circulation, reducing swelling in the feet.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the English ivy tea plant have diuretic properties, which can help reduce fluid retention and alleviate swelling in the feet.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 English ivy leaves and rinse them thoroughly under running water to remove dirt and bacteria.
Crush or chop the leaves finely to release their active compounds, then place them in a pot and add 2 cups of water.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the leaves, and let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 1-2 cups of the English ivy tea daily, preferably before meals, to reduce swelling in the feet.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tea helps with swollen feet because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and relieve pain. This herbal tea contains bioactive compounds such as hypericin and hyperforin, which can improve blood flow and reduce swelling. Additionally, St. John's wort tea has been shown to have diuretic effects, which can help eliminate excess fluid from the body and reduce swelling in the feet. Drinking St. John's wort tea regularly may help alleviate symptoms of swollen feet and promote overall foot health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: St. John's wort tea helps reduce inflammation, which is important for swollen feet as it can alleviate the swelling and discomfort caused by inflammation. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin and hyperforin.

Analgesic: St. John's wort tea has pain-relieving properties, which can help manage the pain often associated with swollen feet. Hypericin and hyperforin are the active constituents that contribute to this medicinal property.

Venotonic: St. John's wort tea can help improve venous tone and reduce venous insufficiency, which is important for swollen feet as it can help improve circulation and reduce swelling. The active constituent responsible for this property is rutin.

Diuretic: St. John's wort tea has diuretic properties, which can help reduce fluid retention and swelling in the feet. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids.

Antioxidant: St. John's wort tea has antioxidant properties, which can help protect cells from damage and reduce inflammation. This property is important for swollen feet as it can help promote healing and reduce the risk of further damage. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of St. John's Wort are used in making medicinal tea for swollen feet due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling.
Leaves: The leaves of St. John's Wort contain compounds that can improve blood flow, making them useful in treating swollen feet.
Aerial Parts: Not included as per the instruction.
Roots: Not used in making medicinal tea for swollen feet.
Rhizomes: Not used in making medicinal tea for swollen feet.
Stems: Not used in making medicinal tea for swollen feet.
Seeds: Not used in making medicinal tea for swollen feet.
Buds: Not used in making medicinal tea for swollen feet.
Bark: Not used in making medicinal tea for swollen feet.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 teaspoon of dried St. John's Wort flowers with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes to extract the medicinal compounds.
Strain the tea to remove the spent herbs, retaining only the liquid.
Drink the prepared tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day.
Continue this regimen for several days, or as long as needed, to reduce swelling in the feet.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat swollen feet with herbal teas is by using a combination of anti-inflammatory and diuretic herbs. Choose teas made from herbs such as dandelion, horsetail, or nettle, which have natural diuretic properties that help reduce fluid buildup in the body. Additionally, ginger, turmeric, or cinnamon teas can help reduce inflammation and soothe swelling. Steep the tea in hot water for 5-10 minutes, then let it cool before soaking your feet in it for 15-20 minutes, 2-3 times a day. Remember to stay hydrated and maintain a balanced diet to support overall health and reduce swelling. Always consult with a healthcare provider if swelling persists or worsens.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to swollen feet that are treated with herbal teas include inflammation and water retention. For instance, ginger tea is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, making it an ideal remedy for swelling. Dandelion tea is another option, as it acts as a natural diuretic, helping the body eliminate excess fluid. Peppermint tea can also reduce inflammation, while chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects. However, it's essential to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal remedy, especially for those with underlying health conditions or those taking medication.
